commit_scope: fourth-kernel.form M1: a complete standalone native CLI whose every source byte is emitted by Form recipes (fourth-kernel-emit.fk, proven three-way at 7), carried through clang by fourth_kernel_audit.sh with value parity gating the first measured comparison rows against the three walking kernels.

local_validation.summary: The emission is the cell: all three kernels produce the byte-identical standalone C program (verdict 7 — canonical text, prefix parameterization through the self-calling ackermann frame, deterministic re-emission). The audit compiled the emitted text with clang -O2, proved value parity on fib 28 = 317811, sum 1000 = 500500, ackermann 3 6 = 509 across go/rust/ts walkers and the emitted binary, then measured the rows. The Form-emitted native answers full invocations in ~2 ms at 1.3 MB max RSS from a 33 KB binary; the walkers answer the same recursive recipes in 461-1696 ms (fib 28) at 53-139 MB RSS from 4-21 MB carriers. The walkers stay authoritative for semantics; the emitted binary is the fourth carrier seed, not a replacement.

observed_delta.conditions: arm64 Darwin, clang -O2, full-process invocations (startup included), median over 15/5/3 runs

known_limitations.limitation: fib and sum are lowered to loops in the emitted program while the walkers run the recursive recipes — value parity over integers is the gate (the cross-ISA discipline); op-shape parity is not claimed.
known_limitations.follow_up: M4 walks the recipe tree in Form and emits its lowering, making the emitted shape derivable instead of hand-shaped.

known_limitations.limitation: The ts row carries npx tsx process overhead; it names the carrier honestly (form-kernel-ts (tsx)) rather than a compiled TS runtime.
known_limitations.follow_up: A compiled/bundled TS entry would tighten that row when the comparison starts to matter for TS specifically.

known_limitations.limitation: Walker RSS includes each kernel's full runtime; the comparison is carriers-as-shipped, not allocator micro-accounting.
known_limitations.follow_up: Per-milestone rows (M2 compiler, M3 API host) keep the same carriers-as-shipped framing.

ci_validation.summary: PR gates run the three-way band; the audit script is operational tooling exercised locally and runnable anywhere clang exists.

deploy_validation.summary: No route or production surface changes; form-stdlib additions ride the normal api image rebuild and the lattice ingests fourth-kernel.form on post-merge hook.

e2e_validation.expected_behavior_delta: No route behavior changes. The api image gains form-stdlib/fourth-kernel-emit.fk + its band (synced by deploy), and the lattice gains the fourth-kernel.form cell on post-merge ingest — queryable through the substrate door. The new capability is local-carrier: scripts/fourth_kernel_audit.sh emits, compiles, parity-gates, and measures the Form-emitted native CLI against the three walkers.
test_flows: fourth-kernel-emit-band proves the complete standalone C program is byte-identical three-way, prefix-parameterized through the self-calling ackermann frame, and deterministic (verdict 7).

fourth_kernel_audit.sh proves value parity (fib 28 = 317811, sum 1000 = 500500, ackermann 3 6 = 509) across go/rust/ts walkers and the Form-emitted binary before any timing row counts, then measures wall/RSS/size/startup.
e2e_validation.summary: scripts/fourth_kernel_audit.sh end-to-end: emit via kernel, clang -O2, parity gate, measured rows printed with conditions.

phase_gate.gate: emission proven three-way and parity-gated rows measured
phase_gate.next: M2 native BMF compiler (fkb-image parity gate); M3 native API host (coordinate with codex native-http-stack); M4 lowerer-as-recipe and the emitted walker as validate.sh's fourth arm
